mechanism_narrative: A high absolute level of the Federal Funds Rate indicates restrictive monetary policy, which increases the cost of capital for firms and compresses valuation multiples, leading to negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits positive average 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% over a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a robust and expanding economy, which translates to higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: A signal based on the 3-month change in PAYEMS fails to achieve a positive rank information coefficient (IC) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth (RSAFS) outpaces industrial production growth (INDPRO), it signals an imbalance where demand is exceeding supply. This leads to margin pressure for producers and portends an economic slowdown, causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS > 3-month change in INDPRO
failure_condition: Instances where the retail sales vs. industrial production growth spread is positive are not followed by statistically significant negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 days.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle, low-unemployment regime, further increases in employment are interpreted as the economy overheating. This raises expectations of imminent Fed tightening to combat inflation, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.5%, positive PAYEMS surprises are followed by positive S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects broad-based strength in the manufacturing and production sectors of the economy, signaling healthy corporate activity and future earnings growth, which supports higher equity prices.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in INDPRO has a rolling 3-year correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns that is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a high policy rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a stagflationary environment. The central bank is constrained from easing, leading to a prolonged period of high discount rates and weak growth, which is strongly negative for equities.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when both trigger conditions are met fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown, as it reflects weakening consumer confidence and tighter credit conditions. This negative signal leads to immediate downside risk for equities.
failure_condition: Triggered instances are not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns with a frequency greater than the baseline probability of a negative 5-day return.

mechanism_narrative: An increasing unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a direct and highly visible signal of deteriorating economic conditions, leading to downward revisions of earnings forecasts and causing negative returns in the stock market.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in UNRATE has a rolling 3-year correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns that is positive or statistically insignificant.
